---
title: "Virginia Invasive Plant Species Analysis"
author: "Ray Santiago and Seun Oladipo"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: cosmo
    highlight: tango
    toc: true
    toc_float:
      collapsed: true
      smooth_scroll: true
    toc_depth: 3
    df_print: paged
    fig_width: 10
    fig_height: 6
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Packages

```{r cars, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(scales)
library(patchwork)

# Spatial
library(sf)
library(terra)
library(tmap)

# Climate
library(geodata)

# SDM
library(dismo)        # Bioclim + background sampling
library(randomForest) # RF classifier for SDM
```

# Data Cleaning and Merging

## Virginia invansive plants spreadsheet cleaning

```{r vips-clean, echo=FALSE}
vips_raw <- read_excel("vips-list-oct2024.xlsx", skip = 1)

vips_clean <- vips_raw %>%
  mutate(across(
    c(Mountain, Piedmont, Coastal, Full, Partial, Shade, Hydric, Mesic, Xeric),
    ~ as.integer(!is.na(.)))) %>%
  mutate(`VA Invasiveness Rank` = str_trim(`VA Invasiveness Rank`)) %>%
  drop_na(`Common Name`, `Scientific Name`)
```

## Load plant geodatabase

```{r}
# ── Lifeform lookup ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
lifeform_lookup <- c(
  "Ailanthus altissima"   = "Tree",       "Microstegium vimineum"  = "Graminoid",
  "Rosa multiflora"       = "Shrub",      "Elaeagnus umbellata"    = "Shrub",
  "Berberis thunbergii"   = "Shrub",      "Alliaria petiolata"     = "Forb/Herb",
  "Lonicera japonica"     = "Vine",       "Lonicera maackii"       = "Shrub",
  "Lonicera morrowii"     = "Shrub",      "Paulownia tomentosa"    = "Tree",
  "Celastrus orbiculatus" = "Vine",       "Ligustrum vulgare"      = "Shrub",
  "Lespedeza cuneata"     = "Forb/Herb",  "Phalaris arundinacea"   = "Graminoid",
  "Tussilago farfara"     = "Forb/Herb",  "Leucanthemum vulgare"   = "Forb/Herb",
  "Securigera varia"      = "Forb/Herb",  "Polygonum cespitosum"   = "Forb/Herb"
  )

# ── Load geodatabase ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
va_bbox <- st_bbox(c(xmin=-83.7, ymin=36.5, xmax=-75.2, ymax=39.5), crs=st_crs(4326))

invasive_sf <- st_read("Bio_InvasivePlantCurrent.gdb/", quiet = TRUE)

cat("Raw records:", nrow(invasive_sf), "\n")

```
```{r}
# — Load geodatabase —
va_bbox <- st_bbox(c(xmin=-83.7, ymin=36.5, xmax=-75.2, ymax=39.5), crs=st_crs(4326))

# Buffer the bbox slightly so wkt_filter doesn't clip edge polygons
va_bbox_buf <- va_bbox %>%
  st_as_sfc() %>%
  st_buffer(0.2) %>%   # ~20km buffer so edge records aren't dropped
  st_as_text()

invasive_sf <- st_read(
  "Bio_InvasivePlantCurrent.gdb/",
  wkt_filter = va_bbox_buf,
  quiet = TRUE
)

cat("Raw records:", nrow(invasive_sf), "\n")
```


```{r invasive-clean}
invasive_va <- invasive_sf %>%
  st_make_valid() %>%                          # skip is_valid check, make_valid is a no-op on valid geoms
  dplyr::filter(!st_is_empty(st_geometry(.))) %>%   # much faster than is_valid
  dplyr::select(
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME, ACCEPTED_COMMON_NAME,
    LIFEFORM_DOMINANT, GIS_ACRES, DATE_COLLECTED,
    DISTANCE_TO_WATER_HORIZONTAL, SHAPE
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME = case_when(
    str_detect(ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME, "Elaeagnus umbellata")  ~ "Elaeagnus umbellata",
    str_detect(ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME, "Polygonum cespitosum") ~ "Polygonum cespitosum",
    TRUE ~ ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME
  )) %>%
  # ---- FAST DEDUP: hash WKT instead of comparing geometries ----
  dplyr::mutate(.geom_hash = sapply(st_as_text(st_geometry(.)), digest::digest)) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME, .geom_hash, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::select(-.geom_hash) %>%
  # ---- rest of your mutates ----
  dplyr::mutate(LIFEFORM_DOMINANT = case_when(
    !is.na(LIFEFORM_DOMINANT)                           ~ LIFEFORM_DOMINANT,
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Ailanthus altissima"   ~ "Tree",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Microstegium vimineum" ~ "Graminoid",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Rosa multiflora"       ~ "Shrub",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Elaeagnus umbellata"   ~ "Shrub",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Berberis thunbergii"   ~ "Shrub",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Alliaria petiolata"    ~ "Forb/Herb",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Lonicera japonica"     ~ "Vine",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Lonicera maackii"      ~ "Shrub",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Lonicera morrowii"     ~ "Shrub",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Paulownia tomentosa"   ~ "Tree",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Celastrus orbiculatus" ~ "Vine",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Ligustrum vulgare"     ~ "Shrub",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Lespedeza cuneata"     ~ "Forb/Herb",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Phalaris arundinacea"  ~ "Graminoid",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Tussilago farfara"     ~ "Forb/Herb",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Leucanthemum vulgare"  ~ "Forb/Herb",
    ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME == "Securigera varia"      ~ "Forb/Herb",
    TRUE                                                ~ "Unknown"
  )) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    GIS_ACRES = ifelse(is.na(GIS_ACRES), median(GIS_ACRES, na.rm = TRUE), GIS_ACRES),
    DISTANCE_TO_WATER_HORIZONTAL = ifelse(
      is.na(DISTANCE_TO_WATER_HORIZONTAL),
      median(DISTANCE_TO_WATER_HORIZONTAL, na.rm = TRUE),
      DISTANCE_TO_WATER_HORIZONTAL)
  ) %>%
  st_transform(crs = 4326)

# Extract coords AFTER transform (same as before)
coords      <- st_coordinates(st_centroid(st_geometry(invasive_va)))
invasive_va <- invasive_va %>%
  dplyr::mutate(lon = coords[, 1], lat = coords[, 2]) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(lon), !is.na(lat),
                between(lon, -83.7, -75.2),
                between(lat,  36.5,  39.5))

rm(invasive_sf); gc()
cat("Records after cleaning:", nrow(invasive_va), "\n")
```


```{r clip-to-virginia}
va_boundary <- geodata::gadm("USA", level = 1, path = "data/") %>%
  sf::st_as_sf() %>%
  dplyr::filter(NAME_1 == "Virginia") %>%
  sf::st_transform(crs = 4326)

invasive_va <- invasive_va %>%
  sf::st_filter(va_boundary)

cat("Records within Virginia:", nrow(invasive_va), "\n")
```


```{r focal-species}
focal_species     <- c("Ailanthus altissima", "Microstegium vimineum", "Rosa multiflora")
invasive_focal    <- invasive_va %>% dplyr::filter(ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME %in% focal_species)
invasive_focal_df <- invasive_focal %>% sf::st_drop_geometry()

invasive_focal_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(n = n(), lifeform = first(LIFEFORM_DOMINANT),
                   mean_acres = round(mean(GIS_ACRES, na.rm = TRUE), 1))
```

## Climate data

```{r climate-load}
va_clim <- worldclim_tile(
  var  = "bio",
  lon  = -79,
  lat  = 38,
  res  = 0.5,
  path = "data/"
)

# Read full tile directly — bypass global() entirely
bio_path <- sources(va_clim[[1]])[1]
va_clim_crop <- rast(bio_path) |>
  crop(ext(-83.7, -75.2, 36.5, 39.5))

# Select and name the 4 layers we need
va_clim_crop <- va_clim_crop[[c(1, 4, 12, 15)]]
names(va_clim_crop) <- c("bio1_mean_temp", "bio4_temp_seasonality",
                          "bio12_annual_precip", "bio15_precip_seasonality")

cat("Layers:", names(va_clim_crop), "\n")
cat("Extent:", as.character(ext(va_clim_crop)), "\n")
cat("CRS:", crs(va_clim_crop, describe = TRUE)$name, "\n")
```

```{r climate-extract}
focal_pts    <- vect(invasive_focal_df[, c("lon","lat")], geom = c("lon","lat"), crs = "EPSG:4326")
climate_vals <- terra::extract(va_clim_crop, focal_pts, ID = FALSE)
invasive_focal_df <- dplyr::bind_cols(invasive_focal_df, climate_vals) %>%
  dplyr::filter(complete.cases(dplyr::select(., starts_with("bio"))))
cat("Records with climate data:", nrow(invasive_focal_df), "\n")
```


```{r predictor-extraction}
# Reset — rebuild from the clean sf object to remove the duplicate columns
invasive_focal_df <- invasive_focal %>% st_drop_geometry()

# Build points SpatVector from lon/lat columns
focal_pts <- invasive_focal_df %>%
  select(lon, lat) %>%
  vect(geom = c("lon", "lat"), crs = "EPSG:4326")

# Confirm row counts match
cat("Occurrence points:", nrow(focal_pts), "\n")
cat("Focal dataframe:  ", nrow(invasive_focal_df), "\n")

# Extract climate values
climate_vals <- terra::extract(
  va_clim_crop,
  focal_pts,
  ID = FALSE
)

# Bind once cleanly
invasive_focal_df <- bind_cols(invasive_focal_df, climate_vals)

# Verify — should have exactly 15 columns, clean names
cat("Columns:", ncol(invasive_focal_df), "\n")
names(invasive_focal_df)

# Check NAs
invasive_focal_df %>%
  select(bio1_mean_temp, bio4_temp_seasonality,
         bio12_annual_precip, bio15_precip_seasonality) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  print()

# Species climate summary
invasive_focal_df %>%
  group_by(ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_temp_C      = round(mean(bio1_mean_temp,        na.rm = TRUE) / 10, 1),
    annual_precip_mm = round(mean(bio12_annual_precip,   na.rm = TRUE), 0),
    temp_seasonality = round(mean(bio4_temp_seasonality, na.rm = TRUE), 0)
  )
```


```{r sdm-analysis}


# ── Climate raster ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
va_clim_crop <- worldclim_tile(var="bio", lon=-79, lat=38, res=0.5, path="data/")
va_clim_crop <- rast(sources(va_clim_crop[[1]])[1]) %>%
  crop(ext(-83.7,-75.2,36.5,39.5))
va_clim_crop <- va_clim_crop[[c(1,4,12,15)]]
names(va_clim_crop) <- c("bio1_mean_temp","bio4_temp_seasonality",
                          "bio12_annual_precip","bio15_precip_seasonality")

# ── Climate extraction onto focal species ─────────────────────────────────────
focal_pts    <- vect(invasive_focal_df[,c("lon","lat")], geom=c("lon","lat"), crs="EPSG:4326")
climate_vals <- terra::extract(va_clim_crop, focal_pts, ID=FALSE)
invasive_focal_df <- dplyr::bind_cols(
  invasive_focal_df %>% dplyr::select(-any_of(names(va_clim_crop))),
  climate_vals
) %>% dplyr::filter(complete.cases(dplyr::select(., all_of(names(va_clim_crop)))))
cat("Focal records with climate:", nrow(invasive_focal_df), "\n")

# ── Background points ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set.seed(2024)
pred_cols <- names(va_clim_crop)
bg_pts <- terra::spatSample(va_clim_crop, size=10000, method="random",
                             xy=TRUE, na.rm=TRUE) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::rename(lon=x, lat=y)

# ── Random Forest SDM ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
rf_models <- lapply(focal_species, function(sp) {
  pres <- invasive_focal_df %>%
    dplyr::filter(ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME==sp) %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(pred_cols)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(presence=1L)
  bg <- bg_pts %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(pred_cols)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(presence=0L)
  df <- dplyr::bind_rows(pres, bg) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(presence=as.factor(presence))
  n <- sum(df$presence==1)
  randomForest(presence~., data=df, ntree=300,
               importance=TRUE, sampsize=c("0"=n,"1"=n))
})
names(rf_models) <- focal_species

# ── Model accuracy ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\n=== Model Accuracy ===\n")
lapply(focal_species, function(sp) {
  oob <- rf_models[[sp]]$err.rate[300,"OOB"]
  cat(sp, "| OOB error:", round(oob,3), "| Accuracy:", round(1-oob,3), "\n")
})

# ── Variable importance ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
var_labels <- c("bio1_mean_temp"           = "Mean Temp (bio1)",
                "bio4_temp_seasonality"    = "Temp Seasonality (bio4)",
                "bio12_annual_precip"      = "Annual Precip (bio12)",
                "bio15_precip_seasonality" = "Precip Seasonality (bio15)")

vi_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(focal_species, function(sp) {
  imp <- importance(rf_models[[sp]], type=1)
  data.frame(species  = sp,
             variable = var_labels[rownames(imp)],
             importance = imp[,"MeanDecreaseAccuracy"])
}))
cat("\n=== Variable Importance ===\n")
print(vi_df %>% dplyr::arrange(species, desc(importance)))

# ── Climate niche per species ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\n=== Climate Niche Summary ===\n")
invasive_focal_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n              = n(),
    mean_temp_C    = round(mean(bio1_mean_temp,      na.rm=TRUE), 1),
    sd_temp        = round(sd(bio1_mean_temp,        na.rm=TRUE), 2),
    mean_precip_mm = round(mean(bio12_annual_precip, na.rm=TRUE), 0),
    sd_precip      = round(sd(bio12_annual_precip,   na.rm=TRUE), 0)
  )

# ── Statistical tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\n=== Kruskal-Wallis: Temperature ===\n")
kruskal.test(bio1_mean_temp ~ ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME, data=invasive_focal_df) %>% print()

cat("\n=== Kruskal-Wallis: Precipitation ===\n")
kruskal.test(bio12_annual_precip ~ ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME, data=invasive_focal_df) %>% print()

cat("\n=== Pairwise Wilcoxon: Temperature ===\n")
pairwise.wilcox.test(invasive_focal_df$bio1_mean_temp,
                     invasive_focal_df$ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME,
                     p.adjust.method="bonferroni") %>% print()

# ── County hotspots ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\n=== Top 10 Counties by Invasion Pressure ===\n")
va_counties <- geodata::gadm("USA", level=2, path="data/") %>%
  sf::st_as_sf() %>%
  dplyr::filter(NAME_1=="Virginia") %>%
  sf::st_transform(crs=4326)

sf::st_join(invasive_va, va_counties) %>%
  sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
  dplyr::count(NAME_2, sort=TRUE) %>%
  dplyr::rename(county=NAME_2, n_invasive=n) %>%
  head(10) %>% print()
```


# **Figures**

## Figure 1: Time Series


```{r figures}
# Define these first — used across all panels
invasive_va_df <- invasive_va %>% sf::st_drop_geometry()
lifeform_colours <- c("Tree"="darkgreen","Shrub"="#e67e22",
                      "Graminoid"="#f1c40f","Vine"="#8e44ad",
                      "Forb/Herb"="#e74c3c","Unknown"="grey60")

# Panel 1: Detections over time
p1 <- invasive_va %>%
  sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(year = lubridate::year(DATE_COLLECTED)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(year), year >= 2015, year <= 2024) %>%
  dplyr::count(year, LIFEFORM_DOMINANT) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = n, colour = LIFEFORM_DOMINANT)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) + geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = lifeform_colours, name = "Lifeform") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2015:2024) +
  labs(title = "Panel 1: Invasive Plant Detections Over Time — Virginia",
       x = NULL, y = "# Occurrence Records") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

# Panel 2: Top 10 species
p2 <- invasive_va_df %>%
  dplyr::count(ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME, sort = TRUE) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n, y = reorder(ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME, n), fill = n)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#9ecfa8", high = "#1c3a24") +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
  labs(title = "Panel 2: Top 10 Invasive Species in Virginia",
       x = "# Occurrences", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"))


p3 <- vips_clean %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(c(Mountain, Piedmont, Coastal),
                      names_to = "region", values_to = "present") %>%
  dplyr::filter(present == 1, !is.na(`VA Invasiveness Rank`)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = region, fill = `VA Invasiveness Rank`)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("High"="red3","Medium"="#c9742a","Low"="#3a7d44"),
                    name = "Invasiveness\nRank") +
  labs(title = "Panel 3: Species by Region & Invasiveness Rank",
       x = NULL, y = "# Species") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

p1 / p2 / p3
```

## Figure 2: Map
```{r Figure 1}

invasive_va_df <- invasive_va %>% st_drop_geometry()

lifeform_colours <- c("Tree"="darkgreen","Shrub"="#e67e22",
                       "Graminoid"="#f1c40f","Vine"="#8e44ad",
                       "Forb/Herb"="#e74c3c","Unknown"="grey60")

fig1 <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = va_boundary, fill = "#f0ede6", colour = "grey50", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(data = invasive_va_df,
             aes(x = lon, y = lat, colour = LIFEFORM_DOMINANT),
             size = 0.8, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = lifeform_colours, name = "Lifeform") +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-83.7,-75.2), ylim = c(36.5,39.5), expand = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Figure 1: Invasive Plant Species Occurrences in Virginia",
       caption = "Source: USFS Bio_InvasivePlantCurrent GDB") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3, alpha = 1)))
print(fig1)
```


Figure 3 - top 15 species bar chart

```{r figure2}

fig2 <- invasive_va_df %>%
  dplyr::count(ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME, LIFEFORM_DOMINANT, sort = TRUE) %>%
  head(15) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n, y = reorder(ACCEPTED_SCIENTIFIC_NAME, n), fill = LIFEFORM_DOMINANT)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = lifeform_colours, name = "Lifeform") +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
  labs(title = "Figure 2: Top 15 Invasive Species by Occurrence Count",
       x = "Number of Records", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"))
print(fig2)
```


## Supplemental: Interactive map
